reforms.description: The tax credit on domestic work was introduced to stimulate employment in the household services sector by providing a deduction for costs incurred when hiring services from enterprises or non-profit organizations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit incentivizes hiring, which can lead to increased employment and economic activity in the household services sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit has been in force since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is a minor adjustment aimed at stimulating a specific sector rather than a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: An income tax credit on household work... was introduced in its present form in 2001.

reforms.description: Finland is considering the introduction of tuition fees for tertiary education, which would apply to all students and be accompanied by a development of the loan system with income-contingent repayments. This reform aims to enhance the responsiveness of higher education institutions to international demand and improve the quality of education offered.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The introduction of tuition fees and improved loan systems is expected to enhance the quality of education and attract international students, thereby increasing the skill level of the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently under consideration and has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the funding structure of higher education in Finland.
reforms.source_quote: While there has been a recent proposal from a working group in the Ministry of Education to charge non-EU foreign students tuition fees from August 2007...
